sections.heading: Parameters
sections.body:
**Coll:**Collection| Parameter          | Type   | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Required? |
| ------------------ | ------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------- |
| \<collection-name> | string | The name of the collection to drop or the wildcard "\*" . You can specify the wildcard "\*" to drop:All collections generated by the wildcard collection function collectionName()The wildcard collection rule | yes       |**Views:**Views| Parameter    | Type   | Description                   | Required? |
| ------------ | ------ | ----------------------------- | --------- |
| \<view-name> | string | The name of the view to drop. | yes       |

sections.heading: Output
sections.body:
**Coll:**CollectionThe command prints the following output if it succeeds. You can
verify the results by running the `show collections` command.
If the command fails, see
[Troubleshoot Errors](adf-drop-collection-views-cmd-errors) for recommended
solutions.```sh
{ "ok" : 1, "ns" : "<database>.<collection>", "nIndexesWas" : 0 }
```where:* `ns` reflects the collection namespace, which includes the
  database name, the dot (`.`) separator, and the collection
  name. For example: `<database>.<collection>`.

* `nIndexesWas` reflects the number of indexes, the value of
  which is  always `0` on Data Federation.**Views:**ViewsThe command prints the following output if it succeeds. You can
verify the results by running the `storageGetConfig` command.
If the command fails, see
[Troubleshoot Errors](adf-drop-collection-views-cmd-errors) for recommended
solutions.```sh
{ "ok" : 1, "ns" : "<database>.<view>", "nIndexesWas" : 0 }
```where:* `ns` reflects the view namespace, which includes the database
  name, the dot (`.`) separator, and the view name. For
  example: `<database>.<view>`.

* `nIndexesWas` reflects the number of indexes, the value of
  which is always `0` on Data Federation.

sections.heading: Drop Collections Examples
sections.body:
The following examples use the `drop` command to remove sample
collections that were mapped to the sample dataset,
[airbnb](https://atlas-data-lake.s3.amazonaws.com/json/sample_airbnb/listingsAndReviews.json)
and
[weather](https://atlas-data-lake.s3.amazonaws.com/json/sample_weatherdata/data.json),
in the AWS (Amazon Web Services) S3 (Simple Storage Service) store.

sections.heading: Basic Example
sections.body:
The following example uses the `drop` command to remove a sample
collection named `airbnb` in a database named `sample` in the
storage configuration.

```json
use sample
db.runCommand({ "drop" : "airbnb"})
```

The previous command prints the following output:

```json
{ "ok" : 1, "ns" : "sample.airbnb", "nIndexesWas" : 0 }
```

sections.heading: Wildcard Example
sections.body:
The following example uses the `drop` command to remove the wildcard
collection function (`collectionName()`) and all collections created
by the wildcard collection function in a database named `sample` in
the storage configuration.

```json
use sample
db.runCommand ({ "drop" : "*" })
```

The previous command prints the following output:

```json
{ "ok" : 1, "ns" : "sample.*", "nIndexesWas" : 0 }
```

sections.heading: Drop Views Example
sections.body:
The following command removes a [view named "listings"](adf-collections-views-create-cmd-egs) on the [airbnb](https://atlas-data-lake.s3.amazonaws.com/json/sample_airbnb/listingsAndReviews.json)
collection in the `sample` database:

```sh
use sample
db.runCommand({ "drop" : "listings" })
```

The previous command returns the following output:

> **Example:** 
>
> ```json
> { "ok" : 1, "ns" : "sample.listings", "nIndexesWas" : 0 }
> ```

sections.heading: Troubleshoot Errors
sections.body:
If the command fails, it returns one of the following errors.

**Reason:**  Namespace (database, collection, or view) does not exist.

```json
{
   ok: 0,
   errmsg: "ns not found",
   code: 26,
   codeName: "NamespaceNotFound"
}
```

**Solution:** Ensure that the namespace specified in the command is
valid and exists in the storage configuration. If necessary, use the
`getStorageConfig` command to retrieve the list of valid databases,
collections, and views in the storage configuration.

**Reason:** Attempting to remove a collection created by the wildcard
collection function (`collectionName()`).

```json
{
   ok: 0,
   errmsg: "cannot drop a collection created from a wildcard",
   code: 26,
   codeName: "NamespaceNotFound"
}
```

**Solution:** Ensure that the collection you are dropping is not an
individual collection dynamically generated by the wildcard collection
function (`collectionName()`). Data Federation does not support
dropping individual collections generated by the wildcard collection
function.
